name: vegan wellington
instructions:
A full vegan wellington plate with mashed potatoes, mushroom gravy, mushroom duxelles, plant-based filets in puff pastry, and pan-roasted carrots. Adapted from possumfodder (Substack) — their 'Beefy Wellington' post, born of watching Hell's Kitchen reruns. Serves 2 as a full plate.

=== 1. MASHED POTATOES ===

INGREDIENTS:
- 3 lb gold potatoes, peeled and cubed
- 1/3 cup plant milk (or dairy if not vegan)
- 1/3 cup vegan sour cream (or dairy)
- 1/2 tsp salt

PROCEDURE:
1. Add cubed potatoes to a large pot and cover with a few inches of well-salted water. Bring to a boil and cook 10-15 min until fork tender.
2. Drain and return to pot. Add plant milk, sour cream, and salt. Mash to desired consistency — they like them a little chunky.

=== 2. MUSHROOM GRAVY ===

INGREDIENTS:
- 8 oz portabella mushrooms (baby bella), diced
- 1/4 cup vegan butter
- 1 tbsp red wine vinegar
- 1/4 cup all-purpose flour (gluten-free if needed)
- 2 no-chicken bouillon cubes
- 1 sprig each fresh rosemary and thyme
- 2 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce (or tamari)
- 1 tsp Gravy Master (browning sauce — omit if unavailable)
- salt to taste
- water or vegetable broth to loosen

PROCEDURE:
1. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add mushrooms and a pinch of salt.
2. Deglaze with a splash (~1 tbsp) of red wine vinegar.
3. Add flour and cook 2-3 min to form a roux, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
4. Whisk in water or broth. Add bouillon cubes, herbs, soy sauce, and Gravy Master.
5. Bring to a boil, reduce to medium-low, and cook until thickened, stirring often. Salt to taste.

=== 3. MUSHROOM DUXELLES ===

INGREDIENTS:
- 8 oz portabella mushrooms (baby bella), diced
- 1 shallot, diced
- 8-10 cloves fresh garlic, minced (yes, that many)
- 2 tsp fresh thyme (or 3/4 tsp dried)
- 2 tbsp vegan butter
- 1/4 tsp salt
- black pepper to taste

PROCEDURE:
1. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms.
2. Once mushrooms are 'sweaty' (released liquid), add shallot.
3. When shallot begins to soften, add garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper.
4. Reduce heat to low. Cook until sufficiently browned, then remove from heat and set aside in a separate bowl.

=== 4. VEGAN FILETS ===

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 vegan filets (Juicy Marbles is the canonical brand; see notes)
- neutral cooking oil

PROCEDURE:
1. Lightly oil a pan and gently sear the filets on all sides.
2. Don't overcook — they finish in the oven. You just want the surface set and lightly browned.

=== 5. BAKE THE WELLINGTON ===

INGREDIENTS:
- vegan puff pastry (gluten-free if needed — Sweet Loren's recommended in the source)
- neutral oil or plant milk for spritzing

PROCEDURE:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (or per puff pastry package).
2. Roll out puff pastry. Use the filets as a guide to trim into strips ~1.5" wide — you want a little overhang.
3. Spoon the duxelles along each strip. Carefully wrap the filet in the pastry.
4. Lightly wet your finger and press the seam to seal. Place seam-side down on a baking sheet.
5. Spritz the pastry with oil or plant milk to promote browning.
6. Bake 15-20 min until the pastry is puffed and deeply golden brown.

=== 6. PAN-ROASTED CARROTS ===

INGREDIENTS:
- 8 oz tops-on carrots (halved lengthwise if fat)
- 1 tsp brown sugar
- 3 cloves fresh garlic, minced
- dash of cayenne pepper (optional)
- salt and pepper to taste
- oil or butter as needed

PROCEDURE:
1. Use the same pan that seared the filets (fond bonus). Add oil or butter if needed.
2. Cook carrots over medium-high heat 10-12 min until lightly browning and starting to soften.
3. Optional: add a few tablespoons of water and loosely cover to steam a bit for softer carrots.
4. Add brown sugar, garlic, cayenne, salt, and pepper. Continue cooking until the garlic is fragrant and the sugar has caramelized.

=== PLATING ===

Spoon mashed potatoes onto the plate. Set the wellington alongside. Ladle mushroom gravy generously over both the potatoes and the wellington. Arrange the pan-roasted carrots as the vegetable component.
notes:
Adapted from possumfodder on Substack — their vegan 'Beefy Wellington' post. A full plate with 6 components (mashed potatoes + gravy + duxelles + seared filets + puff pastry wrap + carrots). Serves 2.

=== JUICY MARBLES FILETS ARE THE CANONICAL PROTEIN ===

The recipe was written specifically for Juicy Marbles filet mignons — a plant-based whole-cut filet product. The whole-cut structure is what makes wellington work; you want a filet-shaped piece that holds together during the sear, the duxelles wrap, and the bake. Ground vegan meat, seitan chunks, and mushroom-based wellingtons all read as different (and lesser) dishes — the whole-cut plant filet is the specific ingredient the recipe is tuned for.

Juicy Marbles is expensive and often needs to be ordered. Suitable substitutes include Chunk Foods filet (comparable quality), a whole large portabella cap (mushroom-wellington territory — different dish, but works), or a homemade seitan roast if you're committed. Real beef tenderloin obviously works if you're going non-vegan.

=== EACH COMPONENT CAN STAND ALONE ===

The 6 components work individually, not just as a plate. Uses to keep in mind:
- MUSHROOM GRAVY — great over polenta, biscuits, or as a general vegan brown-gravy anywhere brown gravy is called for.
- MUSHROOM DUXELLES — filling for tarts, dumplings, or as a spread on toast. This is the wellington's flavor engine.
- MASHED POTATOES — standard vegan mash; works as a side for any braise or roast.
- PAN-ROASTED CARROTS — the brown-sugar-and-garlic finish is worth borrowing for other roasted vegetable dishes.

=== TECHNIQUE NOTES ===

DON'T OVERCOOK THE FILETS BEFORE THE OVEN: the sear is just to set the surface and add browning — not to cook through. The filet finishes in the oven during the puff-pastry bake. Over-searing gives you dry, rubbery filets by the time the pastry is puffed.

DUXELLES SHOULD BE DRY, NOT WATERY: cook the mushroom mixture until it's browned and any released liquid has evaporated. Watery duxelles will leak into the pastry and make it soggy — you want the pastry crisp on all sides.

SEAL THE PASTRY SEAM PROPERLY: wet finger to press the seam is the low-effort version. For more insurance, brush with a bit of plant milk or aquafaba as a 'glue' before pressing. A pastry that opens during the bake dumps the duxelles and looks sad on the plate.

SPRITZ TO BROWN: puff pastry doesn't brown as reliably vegan as with egg wash. Spritz with oil OR plant milk (both work) for surface browning. Aquafaba brushed on also works and gives a slightly glossier finish.

GLUTEN-FREE PATH: use gluten-free all-purpose flour in the gravy and Sweet Loren's (or comparable) gluten-free vegan puff pastry. Everything else is naturally GF. This is one of the more rewarding GF-and-vegan showpiece dishes.

=== SOURCE CONTEXT ===

From possumfodder's July 10, 2026 Substack post. The introduction notes the recipe was inspired by watching Hell's Kitchen reruns — the author had never seen beef wellington on a menu or heard anyone talk about eating it, so they gave the vegan version a whirl. If you're looking to give Gordon Ramsay a cardiac event from halfway across the world (author's stated goal), this recipe is your vehicle.
